name: Asian Orange Chicken
instructions:
1. In a medium saucepan over medium-high heat, combine the water, rice vinegar, lemon juice, soy sauce, and orange juice. Stir in the brown sugar, chopped green onion, orange zest, minced ginger, minced garlic, and red pepper flakes. Bring the mixture to a rolling boil, stirring occasionally to dissolve the sugar. Remove from heat and let the sauce cool for 10 to 15 minutes until it reaches room temperature.

2. Place the chicken pieces in a large resealable plastic bag. Pour half of the cooled sauce over the chicken, making sure all pieces are coated. Seal the bag, pressing out excess air, and refrigerate for at least 2 hours to allow the flavors to penetrate the meat. Transfer the remaining sauce to a container and refrigerate separately.

3. In another resealable plastic bag, combine the flour, salt, and black pepper, shaking to mix the dry ingredients evenly.

4. Remove the marinated chicken from the bag, allowing excess marinade to drip off. Place the chicken pieces into the bag of seasoned flour, seal it, and shake vigorously until each piece is thoroughly coated with the flour mixture.

5. Heat the olive oil in a large skillet over medium heat until shimmering. Add the floured chicken pieces and cook until golden brown and crispy on both sides, about 3 to 4 minutes per side. Transfer the browned chicken to a paper towel-lined plate and tent loosely with aluminum foil to keep warm.

6. Pour the reserved sauce into the same skillet and bring to a boil over medium-high heat. In a small bowl, whisk together the cornstarch and a small amount of water until completely smooth with no lumps. Slowly stir the cornstarch slurry into the boiling sauce, whisking constantly until the sauce thickens to a glossy consistency.

7. Reduce the heat to medium-low and return the chicken to the skillet. Simmer gently, stirring occasionally and coating the chicken with the sauce, until the chicken is cooked through and the internal temperature reaches 165°F (74°C), about 5 minutes. Serve hot over steamed rice and enjoy!
simpleInstructions:
1. Boil sauce ingredients together, then cool to room temperature.

2. Marinate chicken in half the sauce for at least 2 hours, reserve the rest.

3. Shake chicken in seasoned flour until well coated.

4. Pan-fry chicken until golden and crispy on both sides.

5. Boil reserved sauce, thicken with cornstarch slurry, add chicken back, and simmer until cooked through. Serve over rice!
